title: Validation returns 422 for missing title/author, not the 400 in the spec
evidence: main.py:88 raises HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, but BookCreate (main.py:21-25) makes title/author required so pydantic returns 422 first; test_api tests.py:45 asserts 422
suggestion: Either accept 422 as the documented behavior or make fields Optional and rely solely on the explicit 400 guard

title: Explicit empty-required-field 400 guard is only reachable for empty strings
evidence: main.py:88-92 — missing keys are rejected by pydantic (422) before the handler runs; the 400 branch fires only for "" values
suggestion: Remove the redundant guard or move validation entirely to the model

title: No requirements.txt / pyproject.toml pinning fastapi + uvicorn
evidence: workspace has no dependency manifest; README.md:24 instructs a manual `pip install fastapi uvicorn`
suggestion: Add a requirements.txt to make the environment reproducible

title: SQLite database file books.db committed into the workspace
evidence: books.db present (12KB) alongside source; DB_NAME hard-coded at main.py:11
suggestion: Gitignore the DB file / use a temp DB in tests

title: Synchronous sqlite3 calls inside async route handlers
evidence: main.py:86-102 create_book is async but uses blocking sqlite3 connection/execute
suggestion: Acceptable at this scale; use a thread pool or async driver under load

title: Extra standalone smoke-test script beyond the pytest suite
evidence: test_api.py:17 test_all_endpoints walks the full CRUD lifecycle as a runnable script
